name: Concierge: Service Provider (SP) portal — UI parity with broker/agent + client
completionMessage: All REX-456 SP-portal stories are complete!
phaseNote: Dedicated /sp shell is an architectural lock — SP gets its own route tree (NOT a broker/agent view variant). REX-462 re-homes the deferred REX-437 sp_task_view BigQuery slice.
conventions: SP portal is a DEDICATED /sp route tree (architectural lock REX-456) — NEVER a broker/agent view-mode variant; only leaf task-type bodies / dispatcher components are shared
BigQuery: direct @google-cloud/bigquery SDK only — NO Firebase extensions on the BigQuery write path (Tension #2 locked 2026-04-30)
Tenancy key: polymorphic (entityType, entityId) per REX-299 — NEVER brokerageId-only
Event log: single greenfield concierge_events.events table, category-discriminated — NEVER per-stream tables
CRM is Firestore-only — NEVER mirror CRM data to BigQuery; only emit events about CRM changes to the event log
SP visibility is data-layer enforced: SP-scoped Firestore rules (assigned tasks + org messages) + BQ row scoping — NO function-level role-claim gates (locked REX-361)
Linked-client / member gates resolve at read-time: memberUIDs[] for org members, get(contact).userId == auth.uid for linked clients — NEVER a stale client-uid array (REX-452)
Signals: input() / output() — NO @Input() / @Output()
DI: inject() — NO constructor injection
Control flow: @if, @for, @switch — NO *ngIf / *ngFor
NO Angular Material — use @angular/cdk instead
NO shadows — tonal elevation via surface color tokens only
NO 1px borders — surface color shifts only
NO pill shapes — use design system radius tokens only
Tailwind first; fall back to SCSS BEM only for complex layouts or animations Tailwind cannot express; use GSAP only for sophisticated animations that CSS cannot achieve
Concierge uses its own cg-* design library (libs/concierge/design-library) — NEVER libs/shared/ui-design-library (dlc-*)
Testing: Jest only — NEVER Jasmine
Separate files: .ts, .html, .scss, .spec.ts — NEVER inline template/styles
App components: default encapsulation + :host {} for styles
Design library components: ViewEncapsulation.None + host: { class: 'cg-component-name' }

Seed fixtures must satisfy every new guard/condition the story introduces (e.g. SP membership, roleSubtype, sp_task_view rows) in the SAME diff so cross-actor scenarios pass on a fresh /seed-concierge-reset-*

HARD GATE — DO NOT report the story complete (and do NOT hand off to /epic-story-complete) until the corresponding cross-actor scenario from `reference_concierge_cross_actor_preflight.md` (Claude memory) has been EXECUTED end-to-end via `/concierge-cross-actor-test` and the verifier-side state confirms the flow worked through the UI. Generating a test plan document is NOT sufficient; the cross-actor verify phase exists to catch gaps that compile-and-pass-unit-tests does not — those gaps are only visible when a real persona actually drives the flow. If the scenario reveals a gap (missing UI element, missing seed field, broken realtime propagation, wrong guard, etc.), fix it on the current branch per [[actor-testing-fix-gaps-in-place]] and RE-RUN the scenario to PASS. Only after PASS may you report Phase 7 done. REX-464 owns adding SP cross-actor scenario(s) to the catalog; if no runnable scenario exists for this story's slice yet, file/extend one under REX-464 BEFORE shipping (do not just note the gap and proceed).

Name-refactor (epic ARCH-75, completed 2026-06-10): ALL legacy NgPat/ng-pat names were renamed repo-wide to Giga/giga — Jira tickets/specs written earlier may cite stale names. When a referenced file/symbol doesn't exist, apply the mapping before assuming it's missing: files ng-pat-foo.ts → giga-foo.ts; classes/interfaces/types/enums NgPatX → GigaX; fns/consts/actions/feature-keys ngPatX → gigaX; mid-name selectors/factories selectNgPatX / createNgPatX / connectNgPatX → selectGigaX / createGigaX / connectGigaX; SCREAMING tokens NG_PAT_X → GIGA_X; pipe names ngPatDate → gigaDate, ngPatSecondsToMilliseconds → gigaSecondsToMilliseconds; attribute directive ngPatBypassSW → gigaBypassSW; the 'NagPat' typo → Giga too. Fallback: grep the stem after the prefix (e.g. FirestoreCollectionQuery). EXCEPTION: deprecated libs/shared/material + libs/shared/ui-design-library still use NgPat names (excluded from the rename, being deleted) — references to THEIR symbols (NgPatFirebaseLoginService, NgPatSidenav*, NgPatAppIdleOfflineComponent, NG_PAT_AUTH_PROVIDERS_CONFIG, …) are still correct.
epicEvolutionRules: Capture architectural shifts in THIS epic. When a story surfaces a constraint, pivot, or gap, update the subsequent stories that depend on the decision; do not silently absorb the change in the current story, and do not split the work into a separate epic.

Update downstream discovery / research stories with upstream decisions before they are picked up — discovery work should not re-derive choices that have already been settled in earlier stories of this epic.

Add newly-discovered work as CHILD tickets of THIS epic — never as prose in the epic description. Create them 'To Do' if ready to be worked next (`/epic-next`'s JQL only picks up 'To Do' stories), or 'Backlog' if deferred / not-yet-ready. Reference each in the wave plan as a one-line `KEY — summary` entry.

The epic description holds ONLY: the overall description, the wave order (one line per story, `KEY — summary`), and — at most — a few high-level pivot / architectural-lock bullets. NEVER put per-story implementation detail, acceptance criteria, locked-invariant prose, or follow-up specs in the epic; that detail lives in the child ticket whose scope it is (or the linked design docs). Scope must be readable from the child ticket alone. Keep the wave plan and the actual story set in sync as scope evolves — never let them drift, and never let an entry grow past one line.

Seed fixtures must satisfy every new guard / condition the story introduces. When a story adds a new permission gate, role check, status filter, or any other data-driven UI condition, update the corresponding mock + seed fixture in the SAME diff so cross-actor scenarios pass on a fresh `/seed-concierge-reset-*`. Always trace the new guard/condition back to its seed fixture before declaring Phase 6 done.
